evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic is having an adverse impact on our operations and financial performance, as well as on the operations and financial performance of many of our customers and suppliers.

In response, we have been monitoring and continue to actively monitor the impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on all aspects of our business and geographies, including through a cross-functional crisis management team established to respond to the changing conditions.

The forward-looking statements included in this Quarterly Report are based on our current expectations, projections, estimates and assumptions. These statements are only predictions, not guarantees. factors.summary: Labor costs are experiencing mixed trends, with cost-saving measures partially offsetting wage inflation pressure in some areas, while in others, labor costs are increasing due to higher SG&A expenses and related factors.

evidence.text: We have also engaged in a number of cost savings measures in order to help mitigate certain of the adverse effects of the COVID-19 pandemic on our financial results, including a freeze on all non-essential open employment requisitions, cancellation of merit-based payroll increases for 2020, reduction of capital expenditures to below $60 million and cuts in other discretionary spending. Together, we are planning approximately $100 million of cost reductions in 2020 as compared to 2019, due in large part to the effects of COVID-19.

SG&A for the three months ended March 31, 2020 increased by $36.8 million, or 30.1%, as compared with the same period in 2019. factors.summary: The company is experiencing a negative margin trajectory, with both gross and operating margins compressing due to lower gross profit and increased SG&A expenses. The decrease in gross profit margin is attributed to factors such as underutilized manufacturing capacity due to the COVID-19 pandemic, increased realignment charges, and a shift in sales mix towards lower-margin original equipment sales. Additionally, the Flowserve Pump Division specifically saw a significant decline in operating margin from 13.2% to 6.2%, indicating further pressure on profitability.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin for the three months ended March 31, 2020 of 29.7% decreased from 33.0% for the same period in 2019. The decrease in gross profit margin was primarily due to the unfavorable impact of underutilized capacity from the COVID-19 pandemic resulting in $8.4 million of manufacturing costs being expensed and other related costs, increased realignment charges associated with our Realignment Programs and a sales mix shift to lower margin original equipment sales.
